figures.caption: Table 1: Impact of the confidence threshold (𝜏) on the compression rate, solution quality, and computational time across three TSPLIB instances. Results are averaged over 100 independent runs, with 𝑁𝐼 = 100, 𝑁𝑆 = 50, 𝛾 = 1 and 𝐾 = 3 held constant.

Table 2: Impact of the initial pool size (𝑁𝐼 ) and sample size (𝑁𝑆 ) on solution quality and computational time across three TSPLIB instances. The values of 𝑁𝑆 correspond to 20%, 50% and 100% of 𝑁𝐼 . The confidence threshold, classical refinement steps and other parameters are held constant (i.e., 𝜏 = 0.8, 𝐾 = 3, 𝛾 = 1).

Table 3: Overall performance of the proposed Hybrid Algorithm across selected TSPLIB instances. The evaluation is performed using the suitable parameter configuration derived from the sensitivity analysis (𝜏 = 0.75, 𝑁𝐼 = 500, 𝑁𝑆 = 250, 𝛾 = 1 and 𝐾 = 3). Results are averaged over 100 independent runs. For the specific large instance rl11849 we employed 𝜏 = 0.3 and averaged the results over 10 runs.

Figure 1: Simplified workflow of the graph contraction process, highlighting the threshold concept. Graphs (a)-(c) depict three TSP solutions sampled from our pool P. The edges highlighted in blue are those that will be fixed. Graph (d) illustrates the fixed edges alongside their respective frequencies. Finally, Graph (e) represents the resulting reduced instance.
